summary:
Diarthrotic joints allow the most freedom of movement due to their fully mobile nature; this contrasts with other joint classifications such as amphiarthrodial and syndiathrodial where there are more limited ranges available anatomically speaking based upon structural design constraints outlined across multiple sources reviewed here today regarding human anatomy & physiology fundamentals related directly back towards answering original query posed about comparative motions between different kinds/joint-types present within musculoskeletal system overall structure/function dynamics.
The summary should be concise while capturing all essential information from Agent2’s response without adding any new content not supported by it. Here is a summarized version adhering strictly to your instructions:

Diatrohric joints permit the widest range of motion because they are completely free-moving, unlike fixed or slightly moveable joints which have less flexibility according to various medical texts focusing on skeletal systems. This allows them to exhibit greater dynamic capabilities than other joint types classified differently based on degrees of movability described extensively throughout these resources. 

This maintains focus solely on critical aspects pertinent to identifying maximum joint movement among distinct categories discussed originally.
